product.name: Daily Journal
product.tagline: A local-first guided journal with dated entries, prompts, moods, and portable backup.

hero.headline: Daily writing without a blank page or a cloud account.
hero.subhead: Daily Journal combines a dated notebook, rotating prompts, mood tracking, and JSON backup in a build that can live as a static site, install as a PWA, and travel into Capacitor wrappers for mobile testing.

summary: Daily Journal is a local-first guided journaling app built around a daily writing ritual: pick a date, draw prompts, capture mood and gratitude, and keep the archive searchable without handing it to a backend.
audience: People who want a private daily writing practice with more structure than a blank note.
Writers who like prompts, mood tracking, and calendar recall in one place.
Makers who need a static-web build now and mobile wrappers later.
coreProblems: Blank-page friction makes daily journaling hard to start and harder to sustain.
Personal archives become difficult to revisit when entries are not anchored to dates and filters.
Local tools often skip backup and restore, which makes portability fragile.
Offline-first journaling apps are rare compared with cloud-led note products.

features.title: Today-first writing flow
features.summary: The main path starts on the current date with streak context, quick mood selection, and direct entry editing.

features.title: Curated prompt system
features.summary: The repo ships 145 prompts across 10 categories, with reroll, favorite, and pin behavior for daily use.

features.title: Calendar and search recall
features.summary: Entries can be revisited by month grid, recent list, free text, tag, mood range, and prompt category.

features.title: Portable local archive
features.summary: IndexedDB is the preferred store, localStorage is the fallback, and JSON export or import keeps the archive movable.

workflows.title: Open the date
workflows.summary: Start with today or jump to another day from the calendar when memory needs a place.

workflows.title: Use prompts as friction control
workflows.summary: Draw one to three prompts, reroll when they are wrong for the day, and pin the ones worth keeping.

workflows.title: Capture the entry in layers
workflows.summary: Write a title and markdown body, then add mood, tags, and a three-part gratitude list.

workflows.title: Retrieve and carry forward
workflows.summary: Search the archive later and export JSON when the record needs to move between builds or devices.

proofPoints.label: Reflection lanes
proofPoints.note: Includes gratitude, goals, anxiety/stress, morning pages, and evening reflection.

proofPoints.label: Storage path
proofPoints.note: README documents IndexedDB with localStorage fallback and JSON backup.

proofPoints.label: Build surfaces
proofPoints.note: The repo contains a static web path, PWA assets, and Capacitor iOS and Android projects.

faq.question: Does the archive leave the device?
faq.answer: Not by default. The repo describes local storage in IndexedDB, with localStorage fallback, plus manual JSON export and import for backup.

faq.question: Is this already on the App Store or Play Store?
faq.answer: No public store listing is evidenced in the repo. Native packaging is part of the build path, but store availability is not claimed here.

faq.question: What keeps the writing flow from feeling empty?
faq.answer: The product leans on prompts, mood tracking, gratitude fields, and calendar recall so each entry begins with some structure.

faq.question: Can the archive move later?
faq.answer: Yes. JSON backup and restore are documented, and the repo's merge logic is designed to avoid duplicate entries by id or date.

catalog.title: Daily Journal
catalog.blurb: A local-first guided journal with prompts, moods, calendar recall, and JSON backup.
catalog.platforms: Web / PWA / iOS wrapper / Android wrapper

repoArchetypeReasoning: README.md describes the app first as a local-first static site and only then notes PWA and Capacitor wrapping.
The main user-facing pages in src/pages are Today, Calendar, Search, and Settings, which strongly indicate a web app workflow.
The repo contains iOS and Android projects, but they are wrappers around the core web build rather than a separate native product surface.
assumptions: Status is marked Pre-release because the repo version is 0.1.0 and no public launch or store listing is present.
Support and privacy URLs are left blank because the repo contains a privacy statement in README.md but no standalone public pages.
The visual gallery uses a custom product anatomy illustration because no screenshots were found in the repo.
missingButUsefulAssets: Real screenshots of the Today, Calendar, Search, and Settings views.
A dedicated support URL for later central-site linking.
A dedicated privacy policy URL if the product is published publicly.
